scope.summary: Preserves actionable state-store, setup-preview, incident, and client-credential causes instead of collapsing them into generic failure responses.
out_of_scope: Changing file-store lock granularity or service reload behavior.
Changing config validation rules, incident artifact contents, or SQL guard policy.

entry_points.name: setup --write
entry_points.trace: setup_config_error_status gives FileStoreError::Locked its own ORACLEMCP_STATE_STORE_LOCKED code and preserves each preview-flow error with a separate code.

entry_points.name: clients revoke
entry_points.trace: client credential store-opening call sites now use client_credential_error_code so a running service lock has the same actionable code.

entry_points.name: incident capture and replay
entry_points.trace: incident capture distinguishes config load, I/O, and absent bundle files; replay distinguishes I/O and absent bundle files instead of reporting a generic policy refusal.

entry_points.name: C6 state-store collision fixture
entry_points.trace: c6_a_store_collision_names_the_lock_holder_and_the_remedy runs setup --write and clients revoke against a separately owned store and requires an actionable lock code rather than the old generic message.

limitations: Scoped offline verification passed: cargo fmt -p oraclemcp -p oraclemcp-core -- --check; cargo test -p oraclemcp --bin oraclemcp a2a_error_statuses_preserve_actionable_causes -- --nocapture; cargo test -p oraclemcp --test c6_cli_vs_running_server -- --nocapture (2 passed).
No live service or live Oracle environment is claimed; C6 supplies a separate process holding the real file-store flock and deterministic tests cover the status mappings.
